title_en: Salah: Prayer — Its Conditions, Pillars, and Times
content_en:
Ibn Qudamah's treatment of salah (prayer) in Al-Kafi is systematic and evidence-based, making it an excellent entry point for the intermediate student seeking to understand both the rulings and their textual foundations. He begins with the overwhelming Quranic and hadith evidence establishing the obligatory nature of the five daily prayers, noting that their obligation was established directly by revelation during the Night Journey (Isra' wal-Mi'raj).

The times of the five prayers are established by the famous hadith of Jibril, in which the Angel Gabriel led the Prophet (peace be upon him) in prayer on two consecutive days to demonstrate the beginning and end times of each prayer (Ahmad, Al-Nasa'i, Abu Dawud). Ibn Qudamah presents each time window with precision: Fajr from true dawn to sunrise; Dhuhr from solar noon to the end of shadow time; 'Asr from shadow doubling to sunset; Maghrib from sunset to the disappearance of the red twilight; 'Isha from twilight's end to midnight (with a permitted extension to dawn).

The conditions for the validity of prayer (shurut al-sihha) are treated thoroughly. Among them, facing the qiblah is examined with particular care. Ibn Qudamah explains that the obligation is to face the direction of the Ka'bah, not the Ka'bah itself, for those at a distance. One who prays in total ignorance of the qiblah direction and cannot determine it must make their best effort (ijtihad), and their prayer is valid even if later found to be off-direction.

The pillars (arkan) of prayer are enumerated with their evidences. The standing position (qiyam) in obligatory prayers is supported by the hadith: 'Pray standing; if you cannot, then sitting; if you cannot, then on your side' (al-Bukhari). The opening takbir is the entry point into the prayer's sacred state (ihram as-salah), after which ordinary speech is impermissible. Al-Fatihah must be recited in every rak'ah, based on the hadith: 'There is no prayer for one who does not recite the Opening of the Book' (al-Bukhari, Muslim).

Bowing (ruku') is performed such that the back is level, the hands grasp the knees, and the spine and neck are straight. Tranquility (tuma'ninah) — remaining still in each position for a moment — is a separate pillar, emphasized by the prophetic command to the man who prayed improperly: 'Go back and pray, for you have not prayed' (al-Bukhari, Muslim). This hadith is the cornerstone proof for the validity conditions of prayer positions.

The prayers of the sick and the traveler (qasr and jama') are discussed with particular sensitivity. The traveler shortens four-rak'ah prayers to two, based on numerous hadiths. Ibn Qudamah notes that qasr is recommended rather than merely permitted, since the Prophet (peace be upon him) consistently shortened his prayers during travel.

Friday prayer and the 'Eid prayers conclude this chapter. Ibn Qudamah is careful to state that the 'Eid prayers are wajib (obligatory) in the Hanbali school — a position Imam Ahmad himself held — based on the Quranic command to pray and sacrifice (108:2) and the consistent practice of the Prophet and the Companions.
content_id:
Pembahasan Ibn Qudamah mengenai salat dalam Al-Kafi bersifat sistematis dan berbasis bukti, menjadikannya titik masuk yang sangat baik bagi pelajar tingkat menengah yang ingin memahami baik ketentuan-ketentuan maupun landasan tekstualnya. Beliau memulai dengan bukti Al-Quran dan hadis yang kuat yang menetapkan kewajiban lima salat harian, mencatat bahwa kewajiban tersebut ditetapkan langsung melalui wahyu selama Isra' Mikraj.

Waktu-waktu kelima salat ditetapkan oleh hadis terkenal Jibril, di mana Malaikat Jibril mengimami Nabi ﷺ dalam salat selama dua hari berturut-turut untuk menunjukkan awal dan akhir waktu setiap salat (Ahmad, An-Nasa'i, Abu Dawud). Ibn Qudamah menyajikan setiap jendela waktu dengan presisi: Subuh dari fajar yang benar hingga matahari terbit; Zuhur dari tengah hari matahari hingga akhir waktu bayangan; Asar dari bayangan berlipat ganda hingga matahari terbenam; Maghrib dari matahari terbenam hingga hilangnya cahaya merah senja; Isya dari akhir cahaya senja hingga tengah malam (dengan perpanjangan yang dibolehkan hingga fajar).

Syarat-syarat keabsahan salat (syurut al-sihha) dibahas secara menyeluruh. Di antaranya, menghadap kiblat dikaji dengan perhatian khusus. Ibn Qudamah menjelaskan bahwa kewajiban bagi yang berada jauh adalah menghadap ke arah Ka'bah, bukan Ka'bah itu sendiri. Seseorang yang salat dalam ketidaktahuan penuh tentang arah kiblat dan tidak dapat menentukannya harus melakukan upaya terbaiknya (ijtihad), dan salatnya sah meskipun kemudian diketahui arahnya melenceng.

Rukun-rukun salat dihitung beserta dalil-dalilnya. Posisi berdiri (qiyam) dalam salat wajib didukung oleh hadis: 'Salalah berdiri; jika tidak mampu, maka duduk; jika tidak mampu, maka berbaring miring' (Bukhari). Takbiratul ihram adalah pintu masuk ke dalam keadaan sakral salat (ihram al-salah), setelahnya percakapan biasa tidak dibolehkan. Al-Fatihah wajib dibaca dalam setiap rakaat, berdasarkan hadis: 'Tidak ada salat bagi yang tidak membaca Pembukaan Kitab' (Bukhari, Muslim).

Rukuk dilakukan sedemikian rupa sehingga punggung sejajar, tangan menggenggam lutut, dan tulang belakang serta leher lurus. Tuma'ninah — tetap diam dalam setiap posisi untuk sesaat — adalah rukun tersendiri, yang ditekankan oleh perintah kenabian kepada laki-laki yang salat dengan tidak benar: 'Kembalilah dan salatkan, karena engkau belum salat' (Bukhari, Muslim). Hadis ini adalah bukti landasan untuk syarat-syarat keabsahan posisi-posisi salat.

Salat bagi orang sakit dan musafir (qasar dan jamak) dibahas dengan kepekaan khusus. Musafir memendekkan salat empat rakaat menjadi dua, berdasarkan banyak hadis. Ibn Qudamah mencatat bahwa qasar adalah yang dianjurkan daripada sekadar yang dibolehkan, karena Nabi ﷺ secara konsisten memendekkan salatnya selama perjalanan.

Salat Jumat dan salat Idul Fitri dan Idul Adha mengakhiri bab ini. Ibn Qudamah berhati-hati untuk menyatakan bahwa salat Id adalah wajib dalam mazhab Hanbali — posisi yang dipegang Imam Ahmad sendiri — berdasarkan perintah Al-Quran untuk salat dan menyembelih (108:2) serta praktik konsisten Nabi ﷺ dan para sahabat.

Ibn Qudamah's treatment of salah (prayer) in Al-Kafi is systematic and evidence-based, making it an excellent entry point for the intermediate student seeking to understand both the rulings and their textual foundations. He begins with the overwhelming Quranic and hadith evidence establishing the obligatory nature of the five daily prayers, noting that their obligation was established directly by revelation during the Night Journey (Isra' wal-Mi'raj).

The times of the five prayers are established by the famous hadith of Jibril, in which the Angel Gabriel led النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم صلى الله عليه وسلم in prayer on two consecutive days to demonstrate the beginning and end times of each prayer (Ahmad, Al-Nasa'i, Abu Dawud). Ibn Qudamah presents each time window with precision: Fajr from true dawn to sunrise; Dhuhr from solar noon to the end of shadow time; 'Asr from shadow doubling to sunset; Maghrib from sunset to the disappearance of the red twilight; 'Isha from twilight's end to midnight (with a permitted extension to dawn).

Bowing (ruku') is performed such that the back is level, the hands grasp the knees, and the spine and neck are straight. Tranquility (tuma'ninah) — remaining still in each position for a moment — is a separate pillar, emphasized by the prophetic command to the man who prayed improperly: 'Go back and pray, for you have not prayed' (al-Bukhari, Muslim).
